summary: ConnectorOpsConnectIntoNearestNodeIn3dCommand — the solver's 3D connect-into-nearest. Uses the system-match resolver as a gate and the canonical ConnectTo API for the actual join.
usecase: Wire up a specific unconnected end to the nearest compatible candidate.
notfor: Bulk connection — use it per end.
precondition: Active document; source element + connector resolvable; at least one candidate connector within radius.
postcondition: Exactly one Connector.ConnectTo call inside one Transaction; no other mutation.
logictree:
1) Resolve source: Resolver_ElementUniqueId required. If Resolver_ConnectorId given, use it; else pick the single unconnected connector (WireError if 0 or multiple).
2) Build candidate pool: every element with a ConnectorManager in the doc, every connector on those, filtered to same Domain + IsConnected=false + not on source element, within MaxSearchRadiusMm.
3) For each candidate, call MatchSystemByName.Evaluate. Drop Abort decisions. Keep Proceed directly; keep ProceedWithWarning only if Resolver_AllowCrossSystemWithWarning == true.
4) Rank by source→candidate Euclidean distance (feet). If empty, WireError "no_candidate".
5) Open Transaction "ConnectorOps::ConnectNearest3d"; source.ConnectTo(nearest); commit. WireRecoverable + RollBack on API throw.
6) Emit connected, target_element_id, target_connector_id, distance_mm, match_decision, reason.

successprompts: Connected to element {target_element_id} / connector {target_connector_id} at {distance_mm} mm.
